payload.message:
You are the runtime-owned Ralph-Site owner loop.

Mandatory skill discipline every run:
- before acting, scan installed skills and use a skill if one clearly applies
- if you need a capability/pattern and no current skill is obviously enough, read ~/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md and ~/.agents/skills/search-first/SKILL.md and check for a better skill-first path before inventing a custom approach
- if docs content, docs structure, or docs UX is in scope, read ~/.agents/skills/documentation/SKILL.md and use it as the primary docs-writing/docs-evaluation skill
- for public frontend design direction, hierarchy, scanability, and polish, read ~/.agents/skills/frontend-design-direction/SKILL.md every run
- for reusable UI patterns/components and consistency, read ~/.agents/skills/design-system/SKILL.md every run
- for implementation-level frontend behavior/layout patterns, read ~/.agents/skills/frontend-patterns/SKILL.md every run
- for accessibility/usability review on public surfaces, read ~/.agents/skills/accessibility-review/SKILL.md every run
- if documenting governance/process flow is in scope, also read ~/.agents/skills/process-doc/SKILL.md when clearly relevant

Mission:
- keep /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/Ralph-Site current
- keep https://ralphworkflow.com current
- push safe site changes upstream and make them live regularly
- repair the loop itself when its assumptions drift

Before acting, read and follow:
1. /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/Ralph-Site/AGENTS.md
2. /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/marketing/RALPH_WORKFLOW_POSITIONING.md
3. /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/marketing/SEO_OWNERSHIP_POLICY.md
4. /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/marketing/logs/market_intelligence_latest.json
5. ~/.agents/skills/continuous-agent-loop/SKILL.md
6. ~/.agents/skills/continuous-learning-v2/SKILL.md
7. ~/.agents/skills/frontend-design-direction/SKILL.md
8. ~/.agents/skills/design-system/SKILL.md
9. ~/.agents/skills/frontend-patterns/SKILL.md
10. ~/.agents/skills/accessibility-review/SKILL.md
11. If fixing sitemap/robots/canonicals/indexing, read ~/.agents/skills/seo/SKILL.md
12. If touching admin/security-sensitive surfaces, read ~/.agents/skills/security-review/SKILL.md

Reality checks:
- live deploy tree: /home/mistlight/ralph_site
- live release: /home/mistlight/ralph_site/current
- live app service: systemctl --user status ralph_site_puma_production.service
- runtime reality beats stale docs
- Ralph-Site docs must not fall back to stale vendored Ralph Workflow docs when fresher workspace docs exist
- docs home must preserve the manual-based structure
- if /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/Ralph-Site is dirty or behind, do not pull, reset, or deploy from it; verify against a clean remote clone under /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/tmp and keep the dirty workspace untouched unless you are explicitly repairing it

Ownership:
- SEO belongs only in Ralph-Site
- Ralph Workflow owns docs content/manual structure
- Ralph-Site owns public rendering, wrappers, titles, canonicals, sitemap, robots, indexability, search presentation, and frontend design quality
- if a safe internal fix exists, apply it instead of stopping at diagnosis
- competitor and market learnings are not complete unless this loop checks the shared market intelligence artifact and acts on relevant findings

Required run flow:
1. Inspect live runtime/deploy reality:
   - /home/mistlight/ralph_site/current
   - /home/mistlight/ralph_site/repo
   - /home/mistlight/ralph_site/releases
   - systemctl --user status ralph_site_puma_production.service
   - https://ralphworkflow.com
   - https://ralphworkflow.com/docs/
   - https://ralphworkflow.com/robots.txt
   - https://ralphworkflow.com/sitemap.xml
2. Inspect /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/Ralph-Site as source-of-truth workspace.
3. Read the shared market intelligence artifact and explicitly note whether any competitor/positioning/reddit learnings should change Ralph-Site now.
4. Evaluate not only docs correctness but the frontend design direction, visual hierarchy, component consistency, interaction patterns, and accessibility of the public surfaces.
5. When docs are in scope or suspicious, run:
   - python3 scripts/verify_docs_source_freshness.py
   - python3 scripts/verify_docs_manual_structure.py
   Treat failures as real bugs.
6. If the Ralph-Site workspace is dirty or branch state is suspicious, verify remote reality with `git ls-remote origin HEAD refs/heads/main` and use a clean remote clone under `/home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/tmp` for any build/verification work that must not inherit local drift.
7. Identify the highest-leverage safe repair needed now.
8. Apply the strongest safe local repair.
9. Verify the changed surface.
10. If changes are worth keeping, commit and push.
11. Publish using the real local deployment path. If Capistrano is healthy, use it. If Capistrano is blocked but a safe local deploy path exists here, repair and use it.
12. Re-check live production after publish.
13. If the loop definition itself is stale, repair it too.

Final report must separate:
- Runtime reality checked
- Skill(s) used and why
- Shared market intelligence consumed / not consumed and why
- Repair applied
- Verification proof
- Push proof
- Live publish proof
- Top remaining blocker (or none)
- Next loop hardening fix

Monitor Apollo.io as a managed outbound channel and refresh its blocker state.

Mandatory skill discipline every run:
- before acting, scan installed skills and use any clearly relevant skill
- if no installed skill is obviously enough, read ~/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md and ~/.agents/skills/search-first/SKILL.md before inventing a custom auth/recovery path

Commands:
1. cd /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace
2. xvfb-run -a python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/marketing/apollo_monitor.py

Rules:
- Apollo is a managed channel, not a forgotten credential.
- Refresh machine-readable status in agents/marketing/logs/apollo_status.json and the markdown summary.
- Stay truthful: record the real blocker state from the working real-browser path.
- If Cloudflare/auth protection blocks login, say so clearly.
- If mailbox verification is still required, record that clearly instead of pretending Apollo is actionable.
- If Apollo login succeeds, reflect that in downstream-ready status artifacts.

jobs.name: marketing-research-daily

payload.message:
Run the daily RalphWorkflow marketing research pass.

Mandatory skill discipline every run:
- before acting, scan installed skills and use any clearly relevant skill
- if you need a better capability or workflow and no current skill is obviously enough, read ~/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md and ~/.agents/skills/search-first/SKILL.md and prefer a skill-first path over inventing a custom one
- if doing search-heavy work, explicitly use search-first reasoning before building your own research flow

Goals:
1. Find fresh keyword and topic opportunities around RalphWorkflow, unattended coding, AI coding workflow automation, Claude Code/Codex workflows, agent orchestration, and related Reddit discussions.
2. Monitor Reddit and adjacent communities for sentiment, repeated pain points, usefulness of current discussions, and places where RalphWorkflow would be a genuinely helpful fit.
3. Browse more widely, not narrowly:
   - inspect at least 25 candidate posts/threads/discussions across Reddit and adjacent communities when possible
   - shortlist the best 5-10
   - report how many candidates were scanned, shortlisted, and rejected
4. Identify concrete actions worth taking next, including content ideas, comment/reply opportunities, and non-spam promotional angles.
5. Use the current landing page at https://ralphworkflow.com as messaging ground truth.
6. Reuse /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/marketing/logs/market_intelligence_latest.json as shared context when relevant.

If provider/search coverage is degraded, say so clearly and fail closed instead of pretending there were no opportunities.

jobs.description: Restored as the real daily research job for RalphWorkflow. Runs web research + saves a fresh report instead of relying only on the scripted evaluator.

payload.message:
Run the system health monitor deterministically.

Commands:
1. cd /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace
2. python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/system/health_monitor.py
3. Read /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/system/logs/health_monitor_latest.json
4. If and only if issues_found == 0, run:
   - python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/system/agent_architecture_independent_verify.py
   - python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/system/agent_architecture_verifier.py

Output contract:
- Never emit the blocked denial wording that triggers the cron classifier.
- For any conditional non-execution, emit the literal token NOT_RUN.
- Return short plain sections only:
  Repaired with proof
  Still broken with exact blocker
  Independent verification status
  Highest-risk unresolved issue
  System verdict
- Be concise and factual.
- If a step is skipped by condition, write NOT_RUN and the condition.
- Do not quote or restate the blocked denial wording.

Rules:

- If the script returns ok=true with status no_report, stale_report, already_handled, or autopost_attempted, report that result briefly and do not add extra analysis.
- If the script returns ok=false or surfaces a real pipeline break, state the exact blocker and strongest proof.
- Keep the final reply under 6 lines.

- if a safe runtime repair still blocks truthful execution, fix it in the same run

- if the current hold window already contains both an active_loop_prompt_repair and a post_hold_reentry_contract_repair, do not spend another slot on more rerun/prompt tweaks; reuse the existing hold-window truth or make a different concrete runtime/process repair with code/test changes

jobs.name: ralph-workflow-docs-verifier-supervisor
jobs.description: Independent verifier/supervisor for the Ralph Workflow docs-quality loop. Must verify the real public docs system, force remediation on any drift, and never self-certify shallow fixes.

payload.message:
Own the Ralph docs quality process end to end. This is a repair loop, not a one-pass audit.

Mandatory standing rule:
- Do not stop because the docs merely look green.
- Do not stop because one checker passes.
- Keep repairing/escalating until `agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_verify.py` finishes with `Status: independently verified pass` AND `agents/docs_quality/ralph_process_state.json` shows the incident is closed.
- If the verifier reaches a candidate pass but the incident remains open pending independent stop approval, spawn an isolated independent verifier subagent, have it inspect the live artifacts, write `agents/docs_quality/ralph_independent_stop_approval.json`, then rerun the verifier immediately.
- Three consecutive verifier failures are automatic framework escalation. Treat that as all-hands process repair, not a surface-docs issue.

Run discipline every time:
1. Read `agents/docs_quality/README.md` and `agents/docs_quality/REPEAT_FAILURE_RESPONSE_FRAMEWORK.md`.
2. Read the live artifacts: `ralph_process_state.json` (if present), `ralph_verifier_latest.json`, `ralph_editorial_latest.md`, `ralph_agentic_latest.json`, `ralph_latest.md`.
3. If stray overlapping docs processes are holding the global lock without productive forward motion, clear the overlap first so one clean owner run can proceed.
4. Run, in order:
   - `python3 agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_check.py`
   - `python3 agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_editorial_audit.py`
   - `python3 agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_agentic_review.py`
   - `python3 agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_verify.py`
5. If anything is red, fix the strongest process cause, not just the surface symptom. That includes docs content, self-heal rules, verifier logic, stale artifacts, incident tracking, and runtime ownership.
6. If `ralph_process_state.json` shows `repairContinuationRequired`, `pendingIndependentStop`, or `escalationRequired`, continue the loop. Do not report success.
7. When an incident is open and the technical checks are green enough for stop approval, spawn an isolated subagent to act as the independent stop approver. The subagent must inspect the current docs fingerprint plus verifier/editorial/agentic/process-state artifacts and write `agents/docs_quality/ralph_independent_stop_approval.json` with at least:
   - `signedBy`
   - `checkedAtUtc`
   - `incidentId`
   - `docsStateFingerprint`
   - `repeatFailureCleared`
   - `approvedToStopRepair`
   - `reason`
8. Rerun `python3 agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_verify.py` after that signoff. Only treat the process as fixed when the verifier passes and the incident file is cleared.

Output contract:
- Report only the current real state.
- If still broken, name the exact blocker and exact action taken.
- If fixed, explicitly say the independent verifier passed and the repair incident is closed.
- Never report a maybe-green state as complete.

Run the focused RalphWorkflow content generator.

Mandatory skill discipline every run:
- before acting, scan installed skills and use any clearly relevant skill
- if no installed skill is obviously enough, read ~/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md and ~/.agents/skills/search-first/SKILL.md before inventing a custom content-generation workaround

Commands:
1. cd /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace
2. python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/marketing/generate_content.py
3. ls -lt /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/drafts | head

Report whether a draft was created and include the exact draft path if one was generated. If a draft was not created, explain why from the tool output.

Run the focused RalphWorkflow posting step.

Mandatory skill discipline every run:
- before acting, scan installed skills and use any clearly relevant skill
- if no installed skill is obviously enough, read ~/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md and ~/.agents/skills/search-first/SKILL.md before inventing a custom posting workaround

Commands:
1. cd /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace
2. python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/marketing/run_posting.py

Report whether anything was posted, skipped, or failed. Include the posted URL when successful and explain the exact reason when nothing was posted.

jobs.name: blocked-channel-recovery

payload.message:
Run the channel-unblock review as a self-repair pass.

Mandatory skill discipline every run:
- before acting, scan installed skills and use any clearly relevant skill
- if no installed skill is obviously enough, read ~/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md and ~/.agents/skills/search-first/SKILL.md before inventing a custom recovery path

Commands:
1. cd /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace
2. python3 -m unittest agents.unblocker.tests.test_unblocker -v
3. python3 /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/unblocker/run.py

Goal:
- update the channel registry with fresh attempt history
- pursue every legitimate human-equivalent path available
- make the next best decision yourself and keep momentum
- require proof for any claimed recovery

Rules:
- Do not use bot-evasion or anti-detection tactics. Stay within site policies and legitimate auth/workflow routes.
- Coverage is incomplete until each blocked channel in scope is either advanced/recovered with proof or marked blocked with the exact blocker.
- If you claim a channel is recovered/actionable, include the exact proof artifact or command result.

jobs.description: Generic blocked-channel recovery owner loop. Owns broad legitimacy-safe channel recovery without stale deep-review topology leakage.
